meta.method: Whole-body per-cell target pools. EPCAM/DLL3/CD3: from per-cell agent densities (Tier-1 native, reliable epithelial/T labels) scaled to organ-true cellularity. CD20/CD19/BCMA/GPRC5D: computed from per-organ B-cell/plasma-cell counts x copies/cell / Vis. IMPORTANT PROVENANCE: the per-organ B/plasma cell counts (LINEAGE_CELLS) are ORDER-OF-MAGNITUDE ESTIMATES chosen for biological plausibility, NOT read from any per-organ table or paper. Only two facts are externally grounded (both web-verified this session): the whole-body total ~3e13 cells with marrow ~90% (Sender/Fuchs/Milo 2016 PLoS Biol 14:e1002533), and normal marrow plasma cells 0.25-2% of nucleated cells (Terstappen flow-cytometry; ksvdl clinical). The estimates were CONSTRAINED to be consistent with those two facts (marrow plasma <2%, marrow the dominant plasma reservoir). The raw Xenium label transfer over-assigned plasma cells (67% vs <2%), so raw agent counts could NOT be used for the B/plasma pool magnitude — hence the estimate-based counts. The per-cell AGENTS still drive per-cell PD/killing (their density heterogeneity is real). These pool magnitudes are a documented modeling assumption to be refined against clinical TMDD data, not a literature readout.
meta.plasma_cell_fraction_marrow: literature 0.25-2% of nucleated cells (Terstappen ScienceDirect; ksvdl <2%); raw label transfer gave 67% (over-labeled) — corrected via absolute counts
meta.caveat: organ interstitial-nM pools invert vs cell count for marrow (large Vis) — the TMDD SINK uses total receptor AMOUNT (nmol) not nM, so bone plasma reservoir contributes correctly to drug consumption
meta.lineage_cells_provenance: ORDER-OF-MAGNITUDE ESTIMATES (not from literature tables); constrained to Sender 2016 total + marrow plasma <2%
meta.spleen_CD20_check: spleen CD20 (MS4A1) emergent pool = 196.6 nM. Prior independent grid-derived anchor was 239 nM; the emergent per-cell-scaled value is 196.6 nM, ~18% below the grid anchor (NOT a 1% match — earlier claim of 241 nM was incorrect). The gap reflects that the emergent pool uses corrected absolute-count B-cell numbers while the grid anchor used raw agent density; both are order-of-magnitude consistent but not calibrated to each other.
